options.label: A
options.text: A force that helps objects move faster

options.label: B
options.text: A force that opposes motion between two surfaces in contact

options.label: D
options.text: The energy needed to push an object

correct_answer: A force that opposes motion between two surfaces in contact
explanation: Friction is a contact force that acts in the opposite direction to motion. It occurs when two surfaces rub against each other and slows objects down or prevents them from moving. Examples include sliding a book across a table or the grip of shoes on the ground.
